title: tls.Config{InsecureSkipVerify: true} disables TLS certificate verification (MITM)

params.passDetail: tls.Config sets InsecureSkipVerify to a safe value — the server's certificate chain and host name are verified.
params.failDetail: tls.Config sets InsecureSkipVerify: true, which turns OFF certificate and host-name verification. Any machine on the path can present a self-signed cert and silently MITM the connection. Remove it (default false); for a private CA set RootCAs instead.
params.absentDetail: No tls.Config{ InsecureSkipVerify: ... } literal in this scope; the secure default (false) applies.

provenance.note: InsecureSkipVerify: true is the Go 'make the TLS error go away' fix that ships to prod and silently disables MITM protection — the Go twin of node's rejectUnauthorized: false.
